retry_reason: debugfs_file_get fails only when the file is being torn down (refcount zero); retrying will not revive the object.
contract_summary: This wrapper ensures a debugfs file's reference count is held for the duration of a file operation. It checks a per-file methods bitmask to allow or deny the operation early. If permitted, it calls debugfs_file_get to take a reference, then invokes the actual file operation (short_fops->name), and finally releases the reference via debugfs_file_put. The reference is acquired and released within the same call; no ownership is transferred to the caller. The caller (typically the VFS) receives only the return value of the underlying operation and must check it for errors. If debugfs_file_get fails, the file is already being destroyed and no further access is possible.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire and release a reference on the debugfs file's active_users refcount around a call to the underlying file operation, with a minimal gate check on methods bitmask.

check.reason: Both debugfs_file_get and debugfs_file_put operate on the dentry derived from filp. The access path from parameter 1 (filp) through f_path.dentry to the refcount field is correct and the type chain matches the callee information. Segment counts match (6 each).
